teachingPoint: Architecture document ingestion: CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE, DEVELOPER_ONBOARDING documents parsed for knowledge extraction. Parser identifies: DNA rules, factory patterns, fabric interfaces, anti-patterns, skills. Parsed metadata stored in xiigen-arch-documents index.
positiveExample: T682 ArchitectureDocumentParser reads CLAUDE.md, identifies Rule 1 (Fabric First), stores as archRule with cfId, description, antiPattern. CrossRef to related rules recorded.
negativeExample: Manual documentation reading. No systematic knowledge extraction. Architecture rules scattered across documents, hard to retrieve.
discriminatingConstraint: Document parsing at ORDER 1. Each rule/pattern extracted with unique ID. Cross-references recorded. Parser deterministic (same doc → same extraction).

teachingPoint: Design decision extraction: Documented decisions (Rule 1, Rule 2, ... Rule 17) parsed to extract: problem statement, solution approach, tradeoff analysis, affected flows, curriculum tier. DR (Design Reasoning) records created from architecture docs. Tier assigned based on doc hierarchy and prevalence.
positiveExample: T682 reads 'Rule 2: No Typed Models (DNA-1). Solution: ALL business data uses Record<string, unknown>.' → Creates DR-RULE-2 record with tier=1 (foundational), problem='Type safety vs flexibility', affects=['FLOW-0A'...'FLOW-40'].
negativeExample: Architecture rules left as documentation only. No structured DR records. Designers cannot query for 'which rules affect FLOW-12?'
discriminatingConstraint: One DR record per documented decision. Tier assigned: 1=foundational, 2=domain-scoped, 3=flow-specific. Problem/solution/tradeoff fields populated from doc text.

teachingPoint: RAG pattern generation from architecture rules: Each extracted architecture rule generates RAG pattern for LLM retrieval. Pattern includes: archetype (GUARD, TRANSFORMER, ANALYZER, etc.), searchKeywords (extracted from rule text), appliesTo flows (from doc cross-references). Patterns stored in xiigen-rag-patterns index.
positiveExample: T682 extracts Rule 1 → generates RAG-FABRIC-FIRST-001: archetype=GUARD, keywords=['fabric', 'interface', 'provider'], appliesTo=['FLOW-0A'...'FLOW-40']. Stored for GraphRAG retrieval.
negativeExample: No RAG pattern generation. Architecture rules documented but not indexed for LLM retrieval. Designers cannot prompt: 'How do I abstract a new provider?'
discriminatingConstraint: One RAG pattern per architecture rule. Archetype determined from rule category. Keywords extracted from rule text. appliesTo populated from cross-references.

teachingPoint: DPO (Direct Preference Optimization) triple creation for model training: From architecture docs, create DPO triples: (instruction, preferred_response, dispreferred_response). Preferred = correct architecture pattern, dispreferred = anti-pattern. Triples stored for model fine-tuning. Tie each triple to curriculum tier and affected flow.
positiveExample: T682 creates DPO triple: instruction='How to fetch Canva text elements?', preferred='Use ICanvaFabric interface via factory', dispreferred='Import Canva SDK directly'. Tier=1. Affects FLOW-41.
negativeExample: Architecture patterns documented but not structured for model learning. Cannot fine-tune model on XIIGen patterns.
discriminatingConstraint: One DPO triple per {rule, anti-pattern} pair. Tier reflects importance. Triples used to fine-tune LLM generation prompts.

description: Architecture document parsing and knowledge extraction. CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE_GUIDE, DEVELOPER_ONBOARDING parsed for: DNA rules, factory patterns, fabric interfaces, anti-patterns. Metadata stored for retrieval.
Design decision extraction from architecture docs. Problem/solution/tradeoff parsing. Curriculum tier assignment (1=foundational, 2=domain, 3=flow-specific). DR records created for knowledge base.
Generate RAG patterns from architecture rules for LLM retrieval. Archetype extraction, keyword generation, flow cross-reference population. Patterns indexed for GraphRAG.

DPO triple generation from architecture docs for model fine-tuning. (instruction, preferred_pattern, dispreferred_antipattern) triples created. Tier and flow affinity tracked. Enables model training on XIIGen patterns.
